=== Quick Fix Inventory ===

[D1] (15:21-15:35) TS377024: Using Effect.runSync inside an Effect is not recommended. Effects inside generators can usually just be yielded. effect(runEffectInsideEffect)
  Fix 0: "Disable runEffectInsideEffect for this line"
  Fix 1: "Disable runEffectInsideEffect for entire file"

[D2] (23:5-23:22) TS377025: Using Effect.runPromise inside an Effect is not recommended. The same runtime should generally be used instead to run child effects.
Consider extracting the Runtime by using for example Effect.runtime and then use Runtime.runPromise with the extracted runtime instead. effect(runEffectInsideEffect)
  Fix 0: "Disable runEffectInsideEffect for this line"
  Fix 1: "Disable runEffectInsideEffect for entire file"
  Fix 2: "Use a runtime to run the Effect"

=== Quick Fix Application Results ===

=== [D1] Fix 0: "Disable runEffectInsideEffect for this line" ===
skipped by default

=== [D1] Fix 1: "Disable runEffectInsideEffect for entire file" ===
skipped by default

=== [D2] Fix 0: "Disable runEffectInsideEffect for this line" ===
skipped by default

=== [D2] Fix 1: "Disable runEffectInsideEffect for entire file" ===
skipped by default

=== [D2] Fix 2: "Use a runtime to run the Effect" ===

--- file:///.src/runEffectInsideEffect.ts ---
// @effect-v3
import { Data, Effect } from "effect"

class DebuggerError extends Data.TaggedError("DebuggerError")<{
  cause: unknown
}> {}

export const program = Effect.gen(function*() {const effectRuntime = yield* Effect.runtime<never>();

  const response = yield* Effect.tryPromise({
    try: () => fetch("http://localhost:9229"),
    catch: (e) => new DebuggerError({ cause: e })
  })
  const data = yield* Effect.promise(() => response.json())

  const websocket = Effect.runSync(Effect.sync(() => new WebSocket(data.url)))
  //                        ^- do not runSync in here

  websocket.onmessage = (event) => {
    const check = Effect.tryPromise({
      try: () => fetch(event.data as string),
      catch: (e) => new DebuggerError({ cause: e })
    })
    Runtime.runPromise(effectRuntime, check)
    //        ^- no runPromise, use a runtime instead
  }
})
